locations.description_short: Hostile mountainous region targeted by Russian raid January 1852.
locations.dramatic_use: Theatre of imperial violence; home of the burned aoul and Shamil's power base.

locations.description_short: Road through tilled fields and meadows toward forest-clad hills.
locations.dramatic_use: Opening war tableau; establishes Butler's romantic view of war.

locations.description_short: Clear rapid stream forded on approach and return from the raid.
locations.dramatic_use: Natural threshold between safety and hostile forest.

locations.description_short: Dark forest from which Chechen horsemen emerge with banners.
locations.dramatic_use: Site of ambush; reveals Shamil's green banner.

locations.description_short: Ever-changing snowy peaks on the lofty horizon like diamonds.
locations.dramatic_use: Lyrical counterpoint to violence; Butler's poetic horizon.

locations.description_short: Valley where Chechen horsemen descend and are repulsed by artillery.
locations.dramatic_use: Combat engagement site; first contact.

locations.description_short: Chechen village burned by Russians; Sado's home, site of atrocity.
locations.dramatic_use: Moral core of the sequence; senseless destruction triggers vengeance.

locations.description_short: Sado's home where Hadji Murat slept, now burned with roof fallen.
locations.dramatic_use: Personalizes the atrocity; Sado's dead son brought here.

locations.description_short: Polluted mosque where Sado's son's body is brought.
locations.dramatic_use: Desecration of sacred space; galvanizes call to Shamil.

locations.description_short: Grandfather's burnt beehives, apricot and cherry trees, scorched.
locations.dramatic_use: Emblem of patient labor destroyed; stoic grandfather motif.

locations.description_short: Village fountain deliberately polluted so water cannot be used.
locations.dramatic_use: Detail of calculated cruelty; water as life taken away.

locations.description_short: Russian frontier fort where Petrov commands and Hadji Murat stays.
locations.dramatic_use: Primary setting of HM's Russian internment; domestic war-theatre.

locations.description_short: Major Ivan Matveich Petrov's quarters, lived in with Marya Dmitrievna.
locations.dramatic_use: Hosts Hadji Murat; crucible of cross-cultural intimacy.

locations.description_short: Marya Dmitrievna rolls pastry with sleeves up when HM arrives.
locations.dramatic_use: First domestic glimpse; Marya's unglamorous power.

locations.description_short: Reception room where Petrov meets HM and farewell tea is served.
locations.dramatic_use: Formal meeting and farewell; burka-gift scene.

locations.description_short: Room Marya assigns to Hadji Murat; Butler visits him there.
locations.dramatic_use: Space of friendship between Butler and HM; songs with Khanefi.

locations.description_short: Where Butler sleeps dreamlessly after the raid-day dinner.
locations.dramatic_use: Closes Ch.16 with innocence/oblivion.

locations.description_short: Steps of the major's house; HM arrives and later is ambushed here.
locations.dramatic_use: Liminal threshold; site of Arslan Khan assassination attempt.

locations.description_short: Dusty street of the fort where HM arrives and departs.
locations.dramatic_use: Public sightline for HM arrivals/departures.

locations.description_short: Russian garrison town HM had been in before arriving at the fort.
locations.dramatic_use: Referenced chain-of-custody for HM's movement.

locations.description_short: Butler's former gambling life; contrast to the Caucasus.
locations.dramatic_use: Counter-world against which war is romanticised.

locations.description_short: Distant homeland whose respect Butler imagines earning.
locations.dramatic_use: Invisible audience Butler performs for.

locations.description_short: Mountain frontier theatre framing Butler's romance of war.
locations.dramatic_use: Overarching landscape of the novella's conflict.

locations.description_short: Shamil's capital aoul where HM's family is held after defection.
locations.dramatic_use: Counter-court to the Russian fort; Shamil's power seat.

aliases: the saklya where the women are kept
locations.description_short: House where HM's mother, two wives and five children are guarded.
locations.dramatic_use: Captivity of HM's family; stakes of his defection.

locations.description_short: Dark fetid pit over seven feet deep holding Yusuf and seven criminals.
locations.dramatic_use: Visceral image of Yusuf's suffering; lever Shamil will pull.

locations.description_short: Courtyard of petitioners guarded by two armed Lesghians.
locations.dramatic_use: Stages Shamil's stony authority as he ignores the crowd.

locations.description_short: Inner court with penthouse where Shamil dismounts; harem behind fence.
locations.dramatic_use: Private/public divide; Aminal peeks from women's side.

locations.description_short: Shamil's private room where Zeidat serves dinner and he prays.
locations.dramatic_use: Shows Shamil's discipline: prayer over desire.

locations.description_short: Room where six elders convene; Yusuf is summoned and threatened.
locations.dramatic_use: Political theatre; Yusuf-Shamil confrontation decides HM's fate.

locations.description_short: Complex of inner/outer courts and harem at Vedeno.
locations.dramatic_use: Seat of imamate authority; mirror to Russian fort.

locations.description_short: Female domain behind the fence where Shamil seeks Aminal.
locations.dramatic_use: Humanizes Shamil; domestic humiliation by Aminal's refusal.

locations.description_short: Porch-like overhang where HM's wives watch Shamil ride in.
locations.dramatic_use: Captive spectators framed under Shamil's gaze.

locations.description_short: Abstract mountain realm of mountaineers' ecstatic adoration for Shamil.
locations.dramatic_use: HM gestures here as true heart-homeland at farewell.

locations.description_short: Aoul where Yusuf lived the easy life of a naib's son.
locations.dramatic_use: Backstory location grounding Yusuf's admiration for Shamil.

locations.description_short: Kumukh aoul friendly to Russians that invited HM to its mosque.
locations.dramatic_use: Trigger for Arslan Khan's blood-feud ambush.

locations.description_short: Mosque HM was invited to but forbidden by princes and Russians.
locations.dramatic_use: Off-screen flashpoint fuelling the assassination attempt.

locations.description_short: City where HM saw theatre and a viceroy's ball; possible assassination site.
locations.dramatic_use: Shamil considers sending assassin here; HM critiques its mores.

notes: Chapter 19 references an off-page battle preceding Shamil's return to Vedeno (treated as 'Battlefield (offscreen)' transition source). Tash-Kichu and its mosque are discussed in Ch.20 but HM never actually goes — kept as locations because they drive the Arslan Khan ambush. 'Mountains' appears both as concrete snowy peaks (Butler's gaze) and as an abstract homeland gesture (HM's farewell) — split into 'Snowy Peaks' vs 'The Mountains (Shamil's domain)'. Grozny and Petersburg are mentioned only as referenced places, not staged. 'Shamil's Compound' used as parent container for the courts/apartments within Vedeno.
